evidence.scientific_clarity: All 4 panels present and correctly assigned: method diagram (A), ablation bars (B), training curves (C), 5x5 heatmap (D); panel labels bold sans-serif upper-left as specified.
evidence.typography: Script explicitly sets font.sans-serif to DejaVu Sans (the rejected default); no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype anywhere in the script.
evidence.palette: Blue/teal/orange/gray palette is largely colorblind-safe; YlOrRd heatmap is directional (good), but no explicit CB-safe palette verification and DejaVu gray used for Baseline curve reduces contrast.
evidence.layout_density: Gridspec(2,3,width_ratios=[2,1,1]) with hspace/wspace set; bottom heatmap spans full width; bar chart x-range 48–70 is appropriate; panel A has open space above the diagram due to fixed axis limits.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches all panels; legend in panel A is plausible but cramped at 6.5pt; no explicit data.json file found (script loads it but file absent), raising reproducibility concern.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts, not Type 42 selectable text; pdf.fonttype=42 is absent from rcParams — text in PDF is not selectable or editable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; data.json is missing from the working directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all four panels, states specific numerical finding (full model 64.7% accuracy), describes convergence in panel C, explains heatmap axes and color encoding, cites data source.

overall_strengths: Well-structured 4-panel layout faithfully follows the gridspec brief with correct width ratios and full-width bottom panel.
Caption is thorough and publication-ready with a concrete numerical claim (64.7%) and clear panel-by-panel descriptions.
overall_weaknesses: Font rendering is bitmap (Type 3) rather than editable vector (Type 42) because pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, and the script explicitly chooses the rejected DejaVu Sans default.
data.json is missing from the working directory, making the figure non-reproducible as submitted despite the caption claiming all data came from it.
